terminal_screen.c(3) Library Functions Manual terminal_screen.c(3)

NAME

terminal_screen.c - Reusable "fixed header + scrolling logs" terminal screen abstraction.

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define TERM_SIZE_CHECK_INTERVAL_US US_PER_SEC_INT
#define MAX_CACHED_LINES 256

Functions


void terminal_screen_render (const terminal_screen_config_t *config)
Render a terminal screen with fixed header and scrolling logs. void terminal_screen_cleanup (void)
Clean up terminal screen resources. session_log_buffer_t * terminal_screen_log_init (void)
Initialize session log buffer for terminal screens. void terminal_screen_log_destroy (void)
Standard log cleanup for terminal screens. void terminal_screen_log_clear (void)
Clear buffered logs for terminal screens. session_log_buffer_t * terminal_screen_get_log_buffer (void)
Get the current session log buffer. void terminal_screen_set_output_fd (int fd)
Set output file descriptor for terminal screens (splash/status).

Detailed Description

Reusable "fixed header + scrolling logs" terminal screen abstraction.

This module extracts the common rendering logic from splash.c and server_status.c into a unified, testable abstraction.

Design:

  • Callback-based architecture for header content generation
  • Automatic log scrolling with line wrapping
  • Thread-safe log buffer management
  • Terminal resize handling
  • Color scheme integration

Definition in file terminal_screen.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

Referenced by terminal_screen_render().

Function Documentation

void terminal_screen_cleanup (void )

Clean up terminal screen resources. Destroys the internal static frame buffer allocated by terminal_screen_render(). Call at program exit or when terminal screen rendering is no longer needed.

Definition at line 586 of file terminal_screen.c.

586                                    {
587   if (g_frame_buf) {
588     frame_buffer_destroy(g_frame_buf);
589     g_frame_buf = NULL;
590   }
591 }




References frame_buffer_destroy().


Referenced by asciichat_shared_destroy().






session_log_buffer_t * terminal_screen_get_log_buffer
  (void )


Get the current session log buffer. Returns the log buffer owned
    by this module, or NULL if not initialized. Used by splash/status screens to
    register with the logger.




Returns


Pointer to log buffer, or NULL


Definition at line 615 of file
  terminal_screen.c.


615                                                            {
616   return g_session_log_buffer;
617 }










void
  terminal_screen_log_clear (void )


Clear buffered logs for terminal screens. Clears all previously
    captured log entries from the buffer. Useful when transitioning between
    screens to start fresh.


Definition at line 609 of file
  terminal_screen.c.


609                                      {
610   if (g_session_log_buffer) {
611     session_log_buffer_clear(g_session_log_buffer);
612   }
613 }






References session_log_buffer_clear().


Referenced by splash_log_clear(),
    ui_mdns_log_clear(), and ui_status_log_clear().






void
  terminal_screen_log_destroy (void )


Standard log cleanup for terminal screens. Destroys the session
    log buffer after screens are done rendering.


Definition at line 602 of file
  terminal_screen.c.


602                                        {
603   if (g_session_log_buffer) {
604     session_log_buffer_destroy(g_session_log_buffer);
605     g_session_log_buffer = NULL;
606   }
607 }






References session_log_buffer_destroy().


Referenced by splash_log_destroy(),
    ui_mdns_log_destroy(), and ui_status_log_destroy().






session_log_buffer_t * terminal_screen_log_init (void
  )


Initialize session log buffer for terminal screens. Creates a new
    session log buffer owned by this module. Splash and status screens use this
    buffer to display logs.




Returns


Pointer to created buffer, or NULL on failure


Definition at line 593 of file
  terminal_screen.c.


593                                                      {
594   if (g_session_log_buffer) {
595     return g_session_log_buffer; // Already initialized
596   }
597 
598   g_session_log_buffer = session_log_buffer_create();
599   return g_session_log_buffer;
600 }






References session_log_buffer_create().


Referenced by splash_log_init(), ui_mdns_log_init(),
    and ui_status_log_init().






void
  terminal_screen_render (const terminal_screen_config_t *
  config)


Render a terminal screen with fixed header and scrolling logs.
    Renders a screen following the pattern:



  
1.

  
Clear screen and move cursor to home (both stdout and stderr)

  
2.

  
Call render_header() callback to draw fixed header

  
3.

  
Calculate log area: rows - fixed_header_lines - 1 (prevent scroll)

  
4.

  
Fetch recent logs from session_log_buffer

  
5.

  
Calculate which logs fit (working backwards, accounting for wrapping)

  
6.

  
Display logs chronologically (oldest to newest, latest at bottom)

  
7.

  
Fill remaining lines to prevent terminal scroll

  
8.

  
Flush stdout




Parameters


Config Module Screen
  configuration (header callback, line count, etc.)


Terminal size is cached internally with 1-second refresh interval
    to avoid flooding error logs if terminal size checks fail repeatedly.


Definition at line 105 of file
  terminal_screen.c.


105                                                                     {
106   // Validate config
107   if (!config || !config->render_header) {
108     return;
109   }
110 
111   // Track startup timing (first 500ms)
112   static bool first_render = true;
113   if (first_render) {
114     g_render_start_time_ns = platform_get_monotonic_time_us() * 1000;
115     first_render = false;
116   }
117 
118   uint64_t now_ns = platform_get_monotonic_time_us() * 1000;
119   uint64_t elapsed_ms = (now_ns - g_render_start_time_ns) / 1000000;
120 
121   // Ensure cursor is visible for log-only UI (splash, status screens)
122   (void)terminal_cursor_show();
123 
124   // Update terminal size (cached with 1-second refresh interval)
125   // Always check on first call (when still at default 24x80) to get correct dimensions immediately
126   uint64_t now_us = platform_get_monotonic_time_us();
127   bool should_check = (g_cached_term_size.rows == 24 && g_cached_term_size.cols == 80) ||
128                       (now_us - g_last_term_size_check_us >= TERM_SIZE_CHECK_INTERVAL_US);
129 
130   if (should_check) {
131     terminal_size_t new_size = {0};
132 
133     // PRIORITY 1: COLUMNS and ROWS environment variables (most reliable, user-controlled)
134     const char *cols_env = getenv("COLUMNS");
135     const char *rows_env = getenv("ROWS");
136     if (cols_env && rows_env) {
137       int cols = atoi(cols_env);
138       int rows = atoi(rows_env);
139       if (cols > 0 && rows > 0) {
140         new_size.cols = cols;
141         new_size.rows = rows;
142       }
143     }
144 
145     // PRIORITY 2: Options-provided width/height (explicit user settings)
146     if (new_size.cols == 0 || new_size.rows == 0) {
147       const options_t *opts = options_get();
148       if (opts && opts->width > 0 && opts->height > 0) {
149         new_size.cols = opts->width;
150         new_size.rows = opts->height;
151       }
152     }
153 
154     // PRIORITY 3: Terminal auto-detection
155     if (new_size.cols == 0 || new_size.rows == 0) {
156       if (terminal_get_size(&new_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
157         // If terminal detection fails, use defaults
158         new_size.cols = 80;
159         new_size.rows = 24;
160       }
161     }
162 
163     if (new_size.cols > 0 && new_size.rows > 0) {
164       g_cached_term_size = new_size;
165     }
166     g_last_term_size_check_us = now_us;
167   }
168 
169   bool grep_entering = log_search_is_entering();
170 
171   // When transitioning out of grep mode, clear the log cache so all logs are redrawn
172   // (grep mode may have shown filtered logs, need to refresh for normal view)
173   static bool last_grep_state = false;
174   if (last_grep_state && !grep_entering) {
175     terminal_screen_clear_cache();
176   }
177 
178   // Also clear cache when pattern changes while still in grep mode (e.g., user backspaces to empty)
179   if (grep_entering && log_search_needs_rerender()) {
180     terminal_screen_clear_cache();
181   }
182 
183   last_grep_state = grep_entering;
184 
185   // Allocate or reuse frame buffer (module-level static to avoid malloc per frame)
186   if (!g_frame_buf) {
187     g_frame_buf = frame_buffer_create(g_cached_term_size.rows, g_cached_term_size.cols);
188     if (!g_frame_buf) {
189       return; // Allocation failed
190     }
191   }
192 
193   frame_buffer_reset(g_frame_buf);
194 
195   if (!grep_entering) {
196     // Normal mode: clear and redraw (all output goes into frame buffer)
197     frame_buffer_clear_screen(g_frame_buf);
198   } else {
199     // Grep mode: overwrite in place, never clear whole screen
200     frame_buffer_cursor_home(g_frame_buf);
201   }
202 
203   // Render fixed header via callback (writes into frame buffer)
204   config->render_header(g_frame_buf, g_cached_term_size, config->user_data);
205 
206   // If logs are disabled, flush and return
207   if (!config->show_logs) {
208     frame_buffer_flush(g_frame_buf);
209     return;
210   }
211 
212   // Measure actual header height by pre-rendering and counting display lines
213   int actual_header_height = config->fixed_header_lines;
214 
215   if (config->render_header) {
216     // Pre-render header to a temporary buffer to measure its display height
217     frame_buffer_t *temp_buf = frame_buffer_create(config->fixed_header_lines + 10, g_cached_term_size.cols);
218     if (temp_buf) {
219       config->render_header(temp_buf, g_cached_term_size, config->user_data);
220 
221       // Measure display height of the rendered header
222       // The buffer contains ANSI codes + text. We count newlines and account for wrapped lines.
223       int measured_height = 0;
224       const char *content = frame_buffer_get_content(temp_buf);
225       size_t buf_len = frame_buffer_get_length(temp_buf);
226       const char *buf_end = content + buf_len;
227       const char *line_start = content;
228 
229       while (content < buf_end) {
230         if (*content == '\n') {
231           // Found end of line - measure this line's display height
232           size_t line_len = content - line_start;
233           char line_buf[1024];
234           if (line_len < sizeof(line_buf)) {
235             strncpy(line_buf, line_start, line_len);
236             line_buf[line_len] = '\0';
237 
238             int line_height = display_height(line_buf, g_cached_term_size.cols);
239             if (line_height <= 0)
240               line_height = 1;
241             measured_height += line_height;
242           }
243           line_start = content + 1;
244         }
245         content++;
246       }
247 
248       if (measured_height > 0) {
249         actual_header_height = measured_height;
250       }
251       frame_buffer_destroy(temp_buf);
252     }
253   }
254 
255   // Calculate log area: total rows - actual header height - 1 (prevent scroll)
256   // When grep search is open, subtract additional row to prevent header bump
257   int log_area_rows = g_cached_term_size.rows - actual_header_height - (grep_entering ? 2 : 1);
258 
259   if (log_area_rows <= 0) {
260     // Terminal too small for logs, flush header and return
261     frame_buffer_flush(g_frame_buf);
262     if (grep_entering) {
263       log_search_render_input_line(g_cached_term_size.cols);
264     }
265     return;
266   }
267 
268   // When grep input is active, reserve 1 row for the grep input line at bottom.
269   // Subtract 1 from log_area_rows to prevent logs from overlapping the grep input.
270   int renderable_log_rows = grep_entering ? (log_area_rows - 1) : log_area_rows;
271 
272   // Fetch and filter logs
273   session_log_entry_t *log_entries = NULL;
274   size_t log_count = 0;
275 
276   if (log_search_is_active()) {
277     asciichat_error_t result = log_search_gather_and_filter_logs(&log_entries, &log_count);
278     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK || !log_entries) {
279       log_entries = SAFE_MALLOC(SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE * sizeof(session_log_entry_t), session_log_entry_t *);
280       if (log_entries) {
281         log_count = session_log_buffer_get_recent(g_session_log_buffer, log_entries, SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE);
282       }
283     }
284   } else {
285     // Get logs from in-memory buffer
286     session_log_entry_t *buffer_entries =
287         SAFE_MALLOC(SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE * sizeof(session_log_entry_t), session_log_entry_t *);
288     if (buffer_entries) {
289       size_t buffer_count = session_log_buffer_get_recent(g_session_log_buffer, buffer_entries, SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE);
290       log_entries = buffer_entries;
291       log_count = buffer_count;
292     }
293   }
294 
295   // Display logs - most recent at bottom, oldest scrolled off top.
296   // Calculate a stable window: show logs that fit in available space, newest last.
297   // This ensures smooth scrolling as new logs arrive (no jumping viewport).
298   // Use renderable_log_rows when in grep mode to account for grep input line.
299   // CRITICAL: account for multi-line logs via display_height() in pre-selection.
300   // Without this, logs overflow and push the header off screen.
301   int first_log_to_display = log_count; // Start past the end (no logs selected)
302   int total_lines_used = 0;
303   int available_rows = grep_entering ? renderable_log_rows : log_area_rows;
304 
305   // Work backwards from newest to oldest log, selecting which fit
306   for (int i = (int)log_count - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
307     int lines_for_msg = display_height(log_entries[i].message, g_cached_term_size.cols);
308     if (lines_for_msg <= 0) {
309       lines_for_msg = 1;
310     }
311     // Check if adding this log would exceed available space
312     if (total_lines_used + lines_for_msg <= available_rows) {
313       total_lines_used += lines_for_msg;
314       first_log_to_display = i; // This is the first (oldest) log we'll display
315     }
316     // Continue checking older logs - they might be shorter and still fit
317   }
318 
319   // If no logs fit, show nothing (just padding)
320   if (first_log_to_display == (int)log_count) {
321     first_log_to_display = log_count; // Will result in 0 logs to display
322   }
323 
324   int logs_to_display = log_count - first_log_to_display;
325 
326   // Log detailed info about what logs are being displayed
327   int logs_displayed_count = logs_to_display;
328   if (elapsed_ms < 500) {
329     log_dev_every(1 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, "[STARTUP_FRAME] t=%llu log_count=%zu first=%d display=%d area=%d",
330                   (unsigned long long)elapsed_ms, log_count, first_log_to_display, logs_displayed_count, log_area_rows);
331   } else {
332     log_debug_every(1 * NS_PER_SEC_INT,
333                     "[HEIGHT_CALC] log_count=%zu first_log_to_display=%d logs_displayed=%d "
334                     "renderable_log_rows=%d",
335                     log_count, first_log_to_display, logs_displayed_count, renderable_log_rows);
336   }
337 
338   if (!grep_entering) {
339     // Normal mode: accumulate everything (header + logs + blank lines) into frame buffer,
340     // then flush atomically. This prevents real-time logs from appearing after the frame,
341     // which would cause the display to scroll and push the header up.
342 
343     // Output logs to frame buffer, counting actual display lines (accounting for wrapping)
344     int terminal_lines_used = 0;
345     for (int i = first_log_to_display; i < (int)log_count && terminal_lines_used < log_area_rows; i++) {
346       const char *msg = log_entries[i].message;
347 
348       // Calculate how many display lines this message will consume when wrapped
349       // Uses display_height() which handles ANSI codes and UTF-8 properly
350       int lines_for_this_msg = display_height(msg, g_cached_term_size.cols);
351       if (lines_for_this_msg <= 0) {
352         lines_for_this_msg = 1;
353       }
354 
355       // Only output if it fits in remaining space
356       if (terminal_lines_used + lines_for_this_msg <= log_area_rows) {
357         frame_buffer_printf(g_frame_buf, "%s\n", msg);
358         terminal_lines_used += lines_for_this_msg;
359       }
360     }
361 
362     // Pad remaining log area with blank lines to maintain fixed frame height.
363     // This ensures every frame has exactly the same height on screen,
364     // preventing flicker caused by dynamic frame height changes.
365     while (terminal_lines_used < log_area_rows) {
366       frame_buffer_printf(g_frame_buf, "\n");
367       terminal_lines_used++;
368     }
369 
370     // Flush entire frame (header + logs + blank lines) in one atomic write
371     frame_buffer_flush(g_frame_buf);
372   } else {
373     // Grep mode: diff-based rendering. Only rewrite lines that changed.
374     // Logs fill renderable_log_rows; the last row is the `/` input line.
375 
376     // Flush frame buffer (containing header) before rendering grep logs
377     frame_buffer_flush(g_frame_buf);
378 
379     // Suppress logging during grep rendering to prevent log messages from
380     // interleaving with escape sequences and appearing as literal text in logs
381     log_level_t saved_level = log_get_level();
382     log_set_level(LOG_FATAL); // Suppress all logging except fatal errors during rendering
383 
384     int log_idx = 0;
385     int lines_used = 0;
386     int fd = frame_buffer_get_screen_output_fd();
387     FILE *output_file = (fd == STDERR_FILENO) ? stderr : stdout;
388 
389     for (int i = first_log_to_display; i < (int)log_count; i++) {
390       const char *original_msg = log_entries[i].message;
391       const char *msg = original_msg;
392 
393       // Strip ANSI codes first to match against plain text
394       char plain_text[SESSION_LOG_LINE_MAX] = {0};
395       strip_ansi_codes(original_msg, plain_text, sizeof(plain_text));
396 
397       // Apply highlighting if match found in plain text
398       size_t match_start = 0, match_len = 0;
399       if (log_search_get_match_info(plain_text, &match_start, &match_len) && match_len > 0) {
400         // Validate match is within bounds
401         size_t plain_len = strlen(plain_text);
402         if (match_start < plain_len && (match_start + match_len) <= plain_len) {
403           const char *highlighted = grep_highlight_colored(original_msg, plain_text, match_start, match_len);
404           if (highlighted && highlighted[0] != '\0') {
405             msg = highlighted;
406           }
407         }
408       }
409 
410       int msg_display_width = display_width(msg);
411       if (msg_display_width < 0) {
412         msg_display_width = (int)strlen(msg);
413       }
414       int lines_for_this = 1;
415       if (g_cached_term_size.cols > 0 && msg_display_width > g_cached_term_size.cols) {
416         lines_for_this = (msg_display_width + g_cached_term_size.cols - 1) / g_cached_term_size.cols;
417       }
418 
419       // When logs are filtered (grep active), they're in a different order than before.
420       // Only use cached pointers when NOT filtering to avoid cache misses on every frame.
421       // This prevents the "spam" effect where all logs redraw constantly during grep searches.
422       bool same_as_before = false;
423       if (!log_search_is_active()) {
424         same_as_before = (log_idx < g_prev_log_count && g_prev_log_ptrs[log_idx] == original_msg);
425       }
426 
427       if (same_as_before) {
428         // Content unchanged - skip past it without rewriting.
429         if (lines_for_this == 1) {
430           fprintf(output_file, "\n");
431         } else {
432           fprintf(output_file, "\x1b[%dB", lines_for_this);
433         }
434       } else {
435         // Content changed - overwrite and clear tail.
436         fprintf(output_file, "%s\n", msg);
437       }
438 
439       if (log_idx < MAX_CACHED_LINES) {
440         g_prev_log_ptrs[log_idx] = original_msg;
441       }
442       log_idx++;
443       lines_used += lines_for_this;
444     }
445 
446     // Fill blank lines up to renderable_log_rows. If there's one line of space left,
447     // render one more partial log (truncated to fit terminal width) instead of blank line.
448     int remaining = renderable_log_rows - lines_used;
449 
450     if (remaining >= 1 && first_log_to_display > 0) {
451       // Render one more log line above the displayed ones, truncated to fit terminal width
452       int prev_idx = first_log_to_display - 1;
453       const char *prev_msg = log_entries[prev_idx].message;
454 
455       int prev_width = display_width(prev_msg);
456       if (prev_width < 0) {
457         prev_width = (int)strlen(prev_msg);
458       }
459 
460       if (prev_width > g_cached_term_size.cols) {
461         // Truncate to fit: progressively test shorter substrings until one fits
462         int target_width = g_cached_term_size.cols - 3; // Reserve space for ellipsis
463         if (target_width <= 0) {
464           fprintf(output_file, "...\x1b[K\n");
465         } else {
466           size_t src_len = strlen(prev_msg);
467           bool found = false;
468 
469           for (size_t truncate_at = src_len; truncate_at > 0; truncate_at--) {
470             char test_buf[SESSION_LOG_LINE_MAX];
471             SAFE_STRNCPY(test_buf, prev_msg, truncate_at);
472             test_buf[truncate_at] = '\0';
473 
474             int test_width = display_width(test_buf);
475             if (test_width < 0) {
476               test_width = (int)strlen(test_buf);
477             }
478 
479             if (test_width <= target_width) {
480               // Found a length that fits
481               fprintf(output_file, "%s...\x1b[K\n", test_buf);
482               found = true;
483               break;
484             }
485           }
486 
487           if (!found) {
488             fprintf(output_file, "...\x1b[K\n");
489           }
490         }
491       } else {
492         // Fits without truncation
493         fprintf(output_file, "%s\x1b[K\n", prev_msg);
494       }
495 
496       remaining--;
497     }
498 
499     // Fill remaining blank lines with color reset to prevent color bleed
500     for (int i = 0; i < remaining; i++) {
501       fprintf(output_file, "\n");
502     }
503 
504     g_prev_log_count = log_idx;
505     g_prev_total_lines = lines_used;
506 
507     // Flush buffered output before rendering grep UI to ensure correct order
508     fflush(output_file);
509 
510     // Atomic grep UI rendering: combine cursor positioning and input line into
511     // a single write to prevent log output from interrupting the escape sequences.
512     // This prevents the race condition where logs appear between the cursor
513     // positioning command and the grep input line rendering.
514     char grep_ui_buffer[512];
515 
516     // Validate terminal size before using it in formatting
517     if (g_cached_term_size.rows <= 0 || g_cached_term_size.rows > 9999) {
518       // Invalid terminal size - skip grep rendering to avoid malformed output
519       return;
520     }
521 
522     int pos = snprintf(grep_ui_buffer, sizeof(grep_ui_buffer), "\x1b[%d;1H", g_cached_term_size.rows);
523 
524     // Validate snprintf succeeded and produced expected output
525     if (pos > 0 && pos < (int)sizeof(grep_ui_buffer) - 256) {
526       // Lock while reading grep state to ensure atomic render
527       // Get the search pattern under mutex protection
528       mutex_t *grep_mutex = log_search_get_mutex();
529       if (grep_mutex) {
530         mutex_lock(grep_mutex);
531         int pattern_len = log_search_get_input_len();
532         const char *pattern = log_search_get_input_buffer();
533         int cursor_pos = log_search_get_cursor_position();
534 
535         // Validate pattern_len and cursor_pos are within reasonable bounds
536         if (pattern_len < 0 || pattern_len > 256) {
537           pattern_len = 0;
538           pattern = NULL;
539         }
540         if (cursor_pos < 0 || cursor_pos > pattern_len) {
541           cursor_pos = pattern_len;
542         }
543 
544         if (pattern_len > 0 && pattern) {
545           int remaining =
546               snprintf(grep_ui_buffer + pos, sizeof(grep_ui_buffer) - (size_t)pos, "/%.*s", pattern_len, pattern);
547           if (remaining > 0 && remaining < (int)sizeof(grep_ui_buffer) - (int)pos) {
548             pos += remaining;
549           }
550         } else {
551           // No pattern yet - just output the slash
552           if (pos + 1 < (int)sizeof(grep_ui_buffer)) {
553             grep_ui_buffer[pos++] = '/';
554           }
555         }
556 
557         // Position cursor at the current edit position within the pattern
558         // After writing "/{pattern}", cursor is at column = 1 + pattern_len + 1
559         // We want it at column = 1 + cursor_pos + 1 (for "/" at column 1, then cursor_pos chars)
560         // So move left by: pattern_len - cursor_pos columns
561         int cursor_offset = pattern_len - cursor_pos;
562         if (cursor_offset > 0) {
563           int remaining =
564               snprintf(grep_ui_buffer + pos, sizeof(grep_ui_buffer) - (size_t)pos, "\x1b[%dD", cursor_offset);
565           if (remaining > 0 && remaining < (int)(sizeof(grep_ui_buffer) - (size_t)pos)) {
566             pos += remaining;
567           }
568         }
569 
570         mutex_unlock(grep_mutex);
571       }
572     }
573 
574     // Write entire grep UI (cursor positioning + input) in single operation
575     if (pos > 0 && pos <= (int)sizeof(grep_ui_buffer)) {
576       platform_write_all(fd, grep_ui_buffer, (size_t)pos);
577     }
578 
579     // Restore logging level after grep rendering completes
580     log_set_level(saved_level);
581   }
582 
583   SAFE_FREE(log_entries);
584 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, terminal_size_t::cols,
    display_height(), display_width(),
    terminal_screen_config_t::fixed_header_lines,
    frame_buffer_clear_screen(), frame_buffer_create(),
    frame_buffer_cursor_home(), frame_buffer_destroy(),
    frame_buffer_flush(), frame_buffer_get_content(),
    frame_buffer_get_length(),
    frame_buffer_get_screen_output_fd(), frame_buffer_printf(),
    frame_buffer_reset(), grep_highlight_colored(),
    options_state::height, log_debug_every, log_dev_every,
    LOG_FATAL, log_get_level(),
    log_search_gather_and_filter_logs(),
    log_search_get_cursor_position(),
    log_search_get_input_buffer(), log_search_get_input_len(),
    log_search_get_match_info(), log_search_get_mutex(),
    log_search_is_active(), log_search_is_entering(),
    log_search_needs_rerender(), log_search_render_input_line(),
    log_set_level(), MAX_CACHED_LINES,

    NS_PER_SEC_INT, options_get(),
    platform_get_monotonic_time_us(), platform_write_all(),
    terminal_screen_config_t::render_header,
    terminal_size_t::rows, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    SAFE_STRNCPY, session_log_buffer_get_recent(),
    SESSION_LOG_BUFFER_SIZE, SESSION_LOG_LINE_MAX,

    terminal_get_size(), terminal_screen_config_t::user_data, and
    options_state::width.


Referenced by ui_status_display(), and
    ui_status_display_interactive().






void
  terminal_screen_set_output_fd (int fd)


Set output file descriptor for terminal screens
  (splash/status).


Parameters


fd File descriptor (STDOUT_FILENO for TTY,
  STDERR_FILENO for piped)


Configures where splash and status screens write their output.
    Should be called before terminal_screen_render() to control output
    destination.


Definition at line 619 of file
  terminal_screen.c.


619                                            {
620   frame_buffer_set_screen_output_fd(fd);
621 }
